title: More graceful handling for `homebrew` cleanup phase.
body:
### Issue Description

While installing Podman Desktop from home-brew, the recipe has found previous Podman Desktop version and tried to uninstall it. I have apparently removed previous installation of Podman Desktop app the macOS way - moved it into Trash. This has caused the installed to stuck.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Install Podman Desktop from Homebrew `brew install Podman-desktop`
2. Remove/Uninstall Podman Desktop (by removing `/Applications/Podman Desktop.app`)
3. Install Podman Desktop from Homebrew


### Describe the results you received

```
brew install podman-desktop
==> Downloading https://formulae.brew.sh/api/formula.jws.json

==> Downloading https://formulae.brew.sh/api/cask.jws.json

==> Upgrading 1 outdated package:
podman-desktop 0.0.4 -> 1.9.1
==> Upgrading podman-desktop
==> Downloading https://github.com/containers/podman-desktop/releases/download/v1.9.1/podman-desktop-1.9.1-arm64.dmg
Already downloaded: /Users/kpiwko/Library/Caches/Homebrew/downloads/6e627eedbecfabe3b6d9da51c2a17d3e0d29a77c60c339ef7903ca9a87c708f5--podman-desktop-1.9.1-arm64.dmg
==> Purging files for version 1.9.1 of Cask podman-desktop
Error: podman-desktop: It seems the App source '/Applications/Podman Desktop.app' is not there.
```

### Describe the results you expected

Something like, simply skipping Podman Desktop.app related execution
```
brew install podman-desktop
==> Upgrading 1 outdated package:
podman-desktop 0.0.4 -> 1.9.1
==> Upgrading podman-desktop
==> Downloading https://github.com/containers/podman-desktop/releases/download/v1.9.1/podman-desktop-1.9.1-arm64.dmg
Already downloaded: /Users/kpiwko/Library/Caches/Homebrew/downloads/6e627eedbecfabe3b6d9da51c2a17d3e0d29a77c60c339ef7903ca9a87c708f5--podman-desktop-1.9.1-arm64.dmg
==> Backing App 'Podman Desktop.app' up to '/opt/homebrew/Caskroom/podman-desktop/0.0.4/Podman Desktop.app'
==> Removing App '/Applications/Podman Desktop.app'
==> Moving App 'Podman Desktop.app' to '/Applications/Podman Desktop.app'
==> Purging files for version 0.0.4 of Cask podman-desktop
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

None

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

macOS 14.4.1, M1 CPU

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Podman-Desktop issues should be reported  here: https://github.com/containers/podman-desktop

However this sounds like a homebrew issue not an upstream problem so you should likely consult with the homebrew and not upstream.
